# Sponsor Strada.MCP

**Model Context Protocol Server for Unity / Strada.Core — Bridge AI Agents to Your Game Projects**

---

## Why Sponsor?

Strada.MCP is an MCP server that exposes Unity Editor and Strada.Core framework capabilities to AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code Copilot, and other MCP-compatible clients). It gives AI agents structured, safe access to your Unity project — reading scenes, generating Strada-native code, running builds, and managing assets — all through the standardized Model Context Protocol.

While generic Unity MCP servers exist, Strada.MCP is purpose-built for the Strada ecosystem. It understands Strada's DI container, ECS architecture, MessageBus patterns, and modular structure, producing code that follows framework conventions rather than generic Unity boilerplate.

Your sponsorship funds continued tool development, protocol compliance, and deeper integration with the Strada ecosystem.

---

## What Your Sponsorship Supports

- **Strada-aware tooling** — MCP tools that understand Strada.Core's DI, ECS, MessageBus, and module architecture, generating framework-native code rather than generic C#.
- **Unity Editor integration** — Scene manipulation, asset management, build pipelines, and test execution exposed as MCP resources and tools.
- **Protocol compliance** — Keeping up with the evolving MCP specification, transport mechanisms, and client compatibility.
- **Security & sandboxing** — Safe file operations, path validation, and confirmation gates to prevent destructive actions from AI agents.
- **Cross-project synergy** — Tight integration with Strada.Core (framework knowledge) and Strada.Brain (agent intelligence), forming a complete AI-assisted Unity development stack.
- **Documentation & client guides** — Setup instructions for every major MCP client, troubleshooting guides, and example workflows.

---

## Sponsorship Tiers

### 🌱 Supporter — $5/month
- My sincere thanks and a mention in the project's sponsors section.
- Early access to release notes and development updates.

### ⚡ Backer — $15/month
- Everything in Supporter.
- Priority on GitHub Issues — bug reports and feature requests reviewed first.
- Access to a sponsors-only discussion channel.

### 🔌 Integrator — $50/month
- Everything in Backer.
- Your name/logo in the README sponsors section.
- Monthly update on new tools, protocol changes, and client compatibility.
- Vote on which MCP tools and resources get built next.

### 🏢 Studio — $150/month
- Everything in Integrator.
- Your studio name/logo prominently displayed in README and documentation.
- Direct support channel for integration and configuration questions (response within 48 hours).
- Early access to pre-release builds and experimental MCP tools.
- Quarterly call to discuss your team's workflow and tool requirements.

### 💎 Enterprise — $500/month
- Everything in Studio.
- Dedicated onboarding session for your team.
- Custom MCP tool development consideration (scoped to Strada ecosystem).
- Co-development credits in release changelogs.

## One-Time Contributions

One-time sponsorships of any amount are welcome and appreciated. Every contribution helps fund the next set of MCP tools and client integrations.

## How Funds Are Used

| Category | Allocation |
|---|---|
| Tool development & MCP compliance | 55% |
| Testing & client compatibility | 20% |
| Documentation & examples | 15% |
| Community & support | 10% |

---

## The Strada Ecosystem

Strada.MCP is one part of a three-project ecosystem designed for AI-assisted Unity development:

| Project | Role |
|---|---|
| **[Strada.Core](https://github.com/okandemirel/Strada.Core)** | High-performance Unity framework (DI + ECS + MessageBus + Reactive + Modules) |
| **[Strada.Brain](https://github.com/okandemirel/Strada.Brain)** | Autonomous AI coding agent with persistent memory, learning, and multi-channel support |
| **Strada.MCP** | MCP server bridging AI clients to Unity/Strada with framework-aware tooling |

Together, they form a complete stack: **Core** provides the architecture, **MCP** exposes it to AI tools, and **Brain** operates autonomously on top of both.
